name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-105
title: validate-meta-contract fails on configuration[2] (prompt-threshold): no values or actions declared
description:
rabbit-cage feature.json configuration[2] (id: prompt-threshold) declares 'storage: json-key' but NEITHER a 'values' block NOR an 'actions' block. The CONFIGURATION schema (contract spec Inv 42) requires oneOf [{required: [values]}, {required: [actions]}] — every configurable MUST declare exactly one mutation style.

REPRODUCTION (HEAD 46bc7ba8):
  python3 .claude/features/contract/scripts/validate-meta-contract.py .claude/features/rabbit-cage
  → configuration[2] must declare exactly one of 'values' or 'actions' (oneOf)
  → exit 1

prompt-threshold is a numeric configurable — user sets the value via /rabbit-config prompt-threshold N. The current schema vocabulary only supports toggle-style (values: {true: ..., false: ...}) and arbitrary action lists. Numeric 'set this integer' isn't modeled cleanly. The configurable is currently functional at runtime (subcommand reaches the rabbit-config dispatcher which writes to settings.local.json env.RABBIT_REFRESH_EVERY) — the failure is schema-side only.

TWO POSSIBLE FIXES:
(a) Extend the configurable to declare an actions block: {'set': {'api': 'set_json_key', 'args': {file: '.claude/settings.local.json', key: 'env.RABBIT_REFRESH_EVERY', value: '{value}'}}}. Aligns with how allowed-tools and bash-allow declare actions for arbitrary user input.
(b) Extend the meta-contract CONFIGURATION schema to admit a third style for free-form/numeric values (e.g., a 'value-storage' style that's just 'set the json-key to the user-supplied value'), then declare prompt-threshold with that style.

(a) is the smaller fix; (b) is the cleaner abstraction. Recommend (a) for the bug fix wave and file (b) as a separate backlog if there's enough collective demand.

DETECTED BY: ws04 CONTRACT-BACKLOG-38 wave during full-suite validation, 2026-05-25.

history.note: TDD cycle complete (session-override + superpowers TDD, cross-feature bundle wave)
